questions.question: Define the following terms with respect to trees: (i) Degree of a node (ii) Height of a tree (iii) Depth of a node
keyPoints: Degree = number of children a node has
Height = longest path from root to leaf (number of edges or levels)
Depth = length of path from root to that node

questions.question: Differentiate between a Full Binary Tree and a Complete Binary Tree with examples.
keyPoints: Full binary tree: every node has 0 or exactly 2 children
Complete binary tree: all levels filled except possibly last, nodes as left as possible
Give visual examples of each type

questions.question: State and prove the relationship between number of nodes and edges in a tree.
keyPoints: A tree with n nodes has exactly (n-1) edges
Proof using induction or considering each node (except root) has exactly one incoming edge
Every edge connects two distinct nodes

questions.question: Explain Binary Search Tree (BST) property with an example.
keyPoints: Left subtree contains values less than parent
Right subtree contains values greater than parent
Property applies recursively to all subtrees
Give example with at least 5 nodes

questions.question: Define and differentiate between internal nodes and leaf nodes in a tree.
keyPoints: Internal node: has at least one child (degree > 0)
Leaf node: has no children (degree = 0)
Also called non-terminal and terminal nodes respectively

questions.question: Explain the various tree traversal techniques (Preorder, Inorder, Postorder, Level Order) with examples and state the order in which nodes are visited.
keyPoints: Preorder: Root, Left, Right
Inorder: Left, Root, Right
Postorder: Left, Right, Root
Level Order: Level by level using queue
Give example tree and show traversal output for each

questions.question: Derive the formula for maximum and minimum number of nodes in a binary tree of height h. Also calculate for h=4.
keyPoints: Maximum nodes = 2^h - 1 (perfect binary tree)
Minimum nodes = h (skewed tree)
For h=4: Max = 15, Min = 4
Explain using geometric series

questions.question: Write and explain the algorithm for inserting a node in a Binary Search Tree. Also analyze its time complexity.
keyPoints: Compare with root, go left if smaller, right if larger
If empty, insert as root
Recursively find correct position
Time complexity: O(h) where h is height, O(log n) for balanced, O(n) worst case

questions.question: Prove that a binary tree with n nodes has exactly (n-1) edges. Also show that the sum of degrees of all nodes equals 2(n-1).
keyPoints: Use induction or contradiction
Every node except root has exactly one edge connecting to parent
Total edges = n-1
Each edge contributes 2 to sum of degrees
